title: How to Cancel SEO in GoHighLevel — Preserve Your Optimizations
description: Learn how to cancel SEO in GoHighLevel while preserving your project data. Step-by-step guide to the cancellation process, frozen projects, and reactivation.
html_content:
Canceling your GoHighLevel subscription doesn't mean losing all the SEO work you've built. One of the platform's smartest features is its automated offboarding flow—a system that intelligently preserves your project data and optimizations while pausing active automation when you leave.

Whether you're scaling back, switching tools, or reassessing your marketing stack, understanding what happens to your SEO projects is critical. This guide walks you through the cancellation process, what "Frozen" project status means, and how to reactivate your campaigns if you change your mind. Plus, get a FREE 30-day GoHighLevel trial (double the standard length) to test-drive the platform risk-free.

📋 In This Guide

  • How to Cancel Your GoHighLevel Subscription (Step-by-Step)
  • What Happens to Your SEO Projects When You Cancel
  • Understanding the "Frozen" Project State
  • Managing Live Optimizations After Cancellation
  • How to Reactivate Your SEO Projects

How to Cancel Your GoHighLevel Subscription (Step-by-Step)

The cancellation process in GoHighLevel is straightforward, but it's important to follow the correct steps to ensure your data is properly handled.

Step 1: Log In to Your Account
Access your GoHighLevel dashboard using your credentials. Navigate to the main menu and locate your account settings.

Step 2: Access Billing Settings
From your dashboard, go to Settings → Billing. This is where all subscription-related options live. You'll see your current plan, billing cycle, and active services including your SEO management module.

Step 3: Locate the Cancel Subscription Option
In the Billing section, look for a "Cancel Subscription" or "Manage Plan" button. If you don't immediately see a cancel option, it may be under a dropdown menu labeled "Plan Details" or "Subscription Actions." Hover over or click these areas to reveal the cancellation option.

Step 4: Select "Cancel SEO" or Full Subscription
GoHighLevel allows you to cancel individual modules (like SEO management) or your entire account subscription. If you only want to pause SEO services while keeping CRM or other tools active, select the module-specific cancellation. For a full account cancellation, choose the complete plan cancellation option.

Step 5: Confirm and Review Terms
A confirmation dialog will appear explaining what happens next—including how your projects will be frozen and any data preservation details. Review this carefully, then confirm your cancellation.

Step 6: Check Your Email
A confirmation email will be sent to your account's primary email address. This email documents your cancellation date, refund eligibility (if applicable), and next steps regarding your projects.

What Happens to Your SEO Projects When You Cancel

This is the critical question most users ask: Will I lose all my SEO work? The answer is no—but with important nuances.

When you cancel your SEO subscription in GoHighLevel, the platform's intelligent offboarding system activates automatically. Here's what occurs:

Immediate Effects: All active automation pauses. Your keyword rankings, backlink tracking, and competitor analysis tools stop running. However, your historical data, reports, and optimization records remain intact in your GoHighLevel account.

Project Data Preservation: All SEO project files, keyword lists, optimization notes, client data, and campaign documentation are preserved. You're not losing years of work—the data stays in your account as a snapshot of what you've accomplished.

Domain & Client Information: Your client websites, domain registrations, and associated metadata remain stored. This makes reactivation seamless if you decide to return to active SEO management.

What Stops Working: Automated ranking checks, scheduled reports, AI-powered optimization suggestions, and any active link-building or content distribution campaigns will cease. Any pending or queued automation tasks are canceled.

💡 Pro Tip

Before canceling, export or screenshot your active campaign data, ranking reports, and optimization recommendations. While GoHighLevel preserves the data, having local backups ensures you have this information accessible for transition to another tool—or for quick reference if you reactivate.

Understanding the "Frozen" Project State

When you cancel your SEO services, GoHighLevel doesn't delete your projects. Instead, they enter what the platform calls a "Frozen" state. This is a deliberate design choice that protects your work.

What "Frozen" Means: A frozen project is essentially paused in time. It remains visible in your GoHighLevel dashboard, but no active processes run against it. You can view historical data, past reports, keyword rankings up to the cancellation date, and all optimization notes—but no new data is being collected or processed.

Can You Still Access Frozen Projects? Yes. You retain full read access to all frozen projects. You can review past SEO strategies, client performance metrics, keyword lists, and competitor analysis data. This is valuable for understanding what worked and what didn't if you ever need to reference the information.

Can You Modify Frozen Projects? No. In the frozen state, you cannot edit project settings, add new keywords, update client information, or make changes to optimization strategies. The project is locked in its current configuration to prevent data corruption or accidental modifications during the dormant period.

How Long Do Projects Stay Frozen? GoHighLevel typically keeps frozen projects accessible for a set period (usually 30-90 days, depending on your account tier). During this window, you can reactivate your subscription and resume management. After this period, projects may be archived or permanently deleted based on your account settings. Check your cancellation confirmation email for specific retention timelines.

Managing Live Optimizations After Cancellation

If you had active optimizations running when you canceled—like pending content updates, backlink campaigns, or rank improvement initiatives—here's what happens:

In-Progress Tasks: Any optimization tasks in progress at the moment of cancellation are halted. Links scheduled to be added aren't added. Content updates scheduled through GoHighLevel's automation aren't pushed. Rank-tracking reports stop collecting data.

Your Clients' Websites Stay Intact: Critically, your clients' actual websites are unaffected. Content you've already published remains live. Backlinks you've already built stay in place. Only new automated processes stop. There's no rollback or removal of work already completed.

Manual Optimizations Continue: If your clients or in-house team are performing manual SEO work (on-page optimization, content creation, link outreach done outside GoHighLevel), those efforts continue uninterrupted. GoHighLevel cancellation only stops the platform's automation—not your human efforts.

Restarting Live Campaigns: If you reactivate your subscription later, you'll need to restart any campaigns you want to resume. GoHighLevel won't automatically pick up where it left off—you'll relaunch optimization strategies, re-enable automation, and reset your campaign parameters.

How to Reactivate Your SEO Projects

Changed your mind? The process to reactivate is clean and straightforward—one of GoHighLevel's major advantages over competitors.

Step 1: Log Back Into Your Account
Access your GoHighLevel dashboard. Even though your subscription is canceled, your account remains accessible during the retention window, and your frozen projects are visible.

Step 2: Navigate to Billing and Select Your Plan
Go to Settings → Billing. You'll see options to reactivate your subscription. Select the same SEO plan tier you previously had, or upgrade/downgrade to a different tier based on your current needs.

Step 3: Confirm Payment Information
Verify or update your payment method if necessary. Confirm billing details and review the plan terms for your reactivation.

Step 4: Reactivate Your Projects
Once your subscription is active again, your frozen projects automatically transition back to active status. You'll see an "Activate" or "Resume" option for each project. Click it to bring the project live.

Step 5: Re-Enable Automations
Return to each project and re-enable the automations you want to run: keyword tracking, rank reporting, optimization recommendations, link building, content distribution, etc. Your previous settings are saved, so reactivation is fast.

Step 6: Resume Monitoring
Once projects are reactivated, GoHighLevel immediately resumes collecting data, running automations, and generating reports. There's no startup delay or learning curve—the system picks up where it left off.

💡 Pro Tip

If you're considering reactivation, do so before your retention window closes. Projects can be archived after 30-90 days, making recovery more difficult. Reactivating early ensures zero data loss and seamless continuity of your SEO management.

Understanding GoHighLevel's cancellation and offboarding process gives you confidence that your SEO work is protected, even if you step back from the platform temporarily. The frozen state preserves your data, automated processes pause cleanly without affecting live client websites, and reactivation is straightforward whenever you're ready to resume.

If you're on the fence about committing to GoHighLevel's SEO management tools, try the platform free for 30 days. You'll get hands-on experience with the SEO module, project management features, and the full suite of automation tools—all without financial risk. If it's not right for your agency, you'll understand exactly how the cancellation process works and how your data is protected.
